title: senml: encoder doesn't validate name concatenation length
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 146-188 (encode function)

RFC 8428 Section 4.2 specifies that the resolved Name (base_name + name) must be a valid URI or URN. The encoder accepts arbitrarily long base_name and name strings, potentially creating:

1. Names exceeding URI length limits (RFC 3986 doesn't specify a limit, but many implementations have practical limits)
2. Names that concatenate to invalid URIs (e.g., malformed UTF-8 after concatenation)
3. Total encoded size exceeding practical buffer limits

While the encoder does handle buffer overflow gracefully (returns BufferTooSmall), it doesn't validate that the semantic content is valid SenML.

Impact: Potentially non-compliant output; receivers may reject or truncate long names; interop issues.

Recommendation: Document that name validation is caller responsibility, or add optional validation.
